title: dactylorhiza praetermissa subsp. osiliensis
intro: Dactylorhiza praetermissa subsp. osiliensis, also known as the Osili Spotted Orchid, is a rare subspecies of orchid native to Estonia. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza praetermissa subsp. osiliensis include its potential to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as asthma and bronchitis, as well as its ability to exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral properties. Traditionally, herbal preparations of the Osili Spotted Orchid are made into infusions, tinctures, and salves, although further research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and safety as a medicinal plant.
